sections.title: Signs Signals and Barricades Inspection Checklist
sections.html:
Signs Signals and Barricades Inspection Checklist

Inspection frequency: Daily or as required by site conditions and task-specific hazards (per 29 CFR 1926.20(b)(2) and WAC 296-155-048(2)).

Item #

Inspection Item

Pass

Fail

Corrective Action Required

1

All signs are legible, clean, and in good repair (per 29 CFR 1926.200(c))

☐

☐

2

Signs are posted at appropriate locations and visible to all affected personnel (per 29 CFR 1926.200(d))

☐

☐

3

Warning signs are orange with black lettering or as specified by standard (per 29 CFR 1926.200(e))

☐

☐

4

Danger signs are red, black, and white with specific hazard wording (per 29 CFR 1926.200(e))

☐

☐

5

Directional signs are properly placed and clearly indicate safe paths (per 29 CFR 1926.200(f))

☐

☐

6

Traffic control signs and devices conform to MUTCD standards (per 29 CFR 1926.200(g))

☐

☐

7

Flaggers are wearing high-visibility apparel (per WAC 296-155-305(2))

☐

☐

8

Flaggers are using appropriate signaling devices (e.g., stop/slow paddles) (per WAC 296-155-305(3))

☐

☐

9

Barricades are properly erected and maintained to prevent unauthorized entry (per 29 CFR 1926.202)

☐

☐

10

Barricades are clearly visible and adequately illuminated at night (per 29 CFR 1926.202)

☐

☐

11

Barricades are stable and resistant to wind or accidental displacement (per 29 CFR 1926.202)

☐

☐

12

Warning lights or reflectors are used on barricades as required (per 29 CFR 1926.202)

☐

☐

13

Access to emergency equipment and exits is not obstructed by barricades (per 29 CFR 1926.202)

☐

☐

14

Temporary traffic control zones are set up according to a traffic control plan (per AGC Ch. 22)

☐

☐

15

All personnel are aware of and following traffic control procedures (per AGC Ch. 22)

☐

☐

Deficiency Log

AGC: Safety and Health Manual Ch. 22 Traffic Control
This form supports compliance with the above regulations. Retain completed forms for minimum 3 years per 29 CFR 1904.33 and WAC 296-27.
